---
title: GTEx
alias: &ALIAS gtex
href: https://gtexportal.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Genotype-Tissue Expression (GTEx) project provides a resource for studying human gene expression and regulation across tissues, linking genetic variants to tissue-specific gene expression phenotypes.

## Key Features

- Profiled gene expression across 54 tissue types from nearly 1,000 post-mortem human donors
- Identified expression quantitative trait loci (eQTLs) linking genetic variants to gene regulation
- Provides raw RNA-seq counts, normalized expression matrices, and splicing QTL data
- Supports tissue-specific regulatory network and co-expression analysis
- Integrates with GTEx portal tools for interactive expression visualization across tissues

## Use Cases in Research

- Identifying tissue-specific expression patterns of genes relevant to infection and immunity
- Using eQTL data to prioritize genetic variants associated with infectious disease susceptibility
- Building tissue-specific gene regulatory networks for systems biology modeling
- Comparing expression of AMR-related genes (transporters, efflux pumps) across human tissues
- Providing reference expression data for benchmarking single-cell deconvolution methods

## Getting Started

Visit [GTEx](https://gtexportal.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
